q: Nuclear energy now provides about 10 % of the world's electricity from about 450 nuclear power reactors.
A nuclear reaction is a process in which a particle or a gamma radiation penetrate into a nucleus and changes its configuration.
In an exothermic reaction, the total mass before a nuclear reaction takes place is greater than the total mass after reaction.
In an endothermic reaction, the total mass before a nuclear reaction takes place is less than the total mass after reaction.
Nuclear fission is a process where a neutron strikes and penetrates a nucleus of uranium-235, and the nucleus splits into two lighter nuclei.
For a chain reaction to be maintained, the uranium-235 has to be above a certain critical mass.
The energy released per atom in a nuclear reaction is about a million times greater than the energy released per atom from a chemical reaction.
Nuclear reactor is a device in which a fission chain reaction can be initiated, maintained and controlled.
The essential elements of a nuclear reactor are fissionable nuclear fuel, moderator, coolant, control rods, reactor vessel and shielding.
Uranium-235 or plutonium-239 can be used as fuel in a nuclear reactor.
Moderator is a material used to speed up fast neutrons released from a fission process.
Graphite and heavy water ($\text{D}_2\text{O}$) are used as moderators.
Coolant is used to extract the heat produced by fission fragments.
Water and carbon dioxide $\text{CO}_2$ gas are used as coolant.
Control rods are used to control the rate of reaction by absorbing neutrons.
Boron and cadmium are used as control rods.
Shielding (or containment structure) is a protective barrier, usually a dense material, that reduces the passage of radiations by absorbing them.
Materials which are radioactive and for which there is no further use are called radioactive waste.
After three or four years in a reactor, spent fuel must be removed and replaced with a new one.
Strontium-90 becomes concentrated in bones and iodine in the thyroid gland.
Plutonium is highly toxic; if breathed in, a very small amount can kill a person.
In an atomic bomb, a controlled chain reaction takes place.
The detonation of an atomic bomb releases enormous amounts of thermal energy, or heat, achieving temperatures of several million degrees Celsius.
Spent nuclear fuel storage is mostly a problem regarding environmental concerns.
Greenhouse gas emissions from nuclear fission power are much larger than those associated with coal, oil and gas.
